args.command:
curl -s "http://localhost:3002/api/sessions/ca2bc88e-7d01-4c66-bd25-235601e1b18a/records" | python3 -c "
import json, sys
records = json.load(sys.stdin)

for r in records:
    ts = r['timestamp'][11:19]
    rt = r['record_type']
    depth = r['depth']
    seq = r['seq']
    p = r.get('payload', {})
    
    detail = ''
    if rt == 'user_message':
        c = p.get('content', '')
        if isinstance(c, str):
            detail = c[:70]
    elif rt == 'assistant_message':
        msg = p.get('message', p)
        content = msg.get('content', [])
        if isinstance(content, list):
            for b in content:
                if isinstance(b, dict) and b.get('type') == 'text':
                    detail = b.get('text', '')[:70]
                    break
        elif isinstance(content, str):
            detail = content[:70]
    elif rt == 'tool_call':
        name = p.get('name', '?')
        inp = p.get('input', {})
        if isinstance(inp, dict):
            if inp.get('command'): detail = f'{name}: {str(inp[\"command\"])[:50]}'
            elif inp.get('file_path'): detail = f'{name}: {inp[\"file_path\"].split(\"/\")[-1]}'
            elif inp.get('pattern'): detail = f'{name}: {inp[\"pattern\"][:40]}'
            elif inp.get('description'): detail = f'{name}: {inp[\"description\"][:40]}'
            elif inp.get('prompt'): detail = f'{name}: {str(inp[\"prompt\"])[:40]}'
            elif inp.get('query'): detail = f'{name}: {inp[\"query\"][:40]}'
            elif inp.get('url'): detail = f'{name}: {inp[\"url\"][:50]}'
            else: detail = f'{name}: {str(inp)[:50]}'
        else:
            detail = name
    elif rt == 'tool_result':
        out = p.get('output', '')
        err = p.get('is_error', False)
        if err: detail = f'ERROR: {str(out)[:50]}'
        else: detail = str(out)[:60]
    elif rt == 'turn_end':
        reason = p.get('reason', '?')
        dur = p.get('duration_ms', '')
        detail = f'{reason} ({dur}ms)' if dur else reason
    elif rt == 'token_usage':
        inp = p.get('input_tokens', 0)
        out = p.get('output_tokens', 0)
        detail = f'in={inp} out={out}'
    elif rt == 'system_event':
        detail = p.get('subtype', '')
    elif rt == 'reasoning':
        c = p.get('summary', p.get('content', ''))
        detail = str(c)[:60] if c else '(redacted)'
    elif rt == 'file_snapshot':
        detail = '(snapshot)'
    
    print(f'{seq:4d}  {ts}  {rt:22s}  {detail}')
" 2>&1 | head -200

curl -s "http://localhost:3002/api/sessions/ca2bc88e-7d01-4c66-bd25-235601e1b18a/records" | python3 -c "
import json, sys
records = json.load(sys.stdin)
print(f'... showing records 200-400 of {len(records)} ...')
print()
for r in records[200:400]:
    ts = r['timestamp'][11:19]
    rt = r['record_type']
    seq = r['seq']
    p = r.get('payload', {})
    
    detail = ''
    if rt == 'user_message':
        c = p.get('content', '')
        if isinstance(c, str): detail = c[:70]
    elif rt == 'assistant_message':
        msg = p.get('message', p)
        content = msg.get('content', [])
        if isinstance(content, list):
            for b in content:
                if isinstance(b, dict) and b.get('type') == 'text':
                    detail = b.get('text', '')[:70]
                    break
    elif rt == 'tool_call':
        name = p.get('name', '?')
        inp = p.get('input', {})
        if isinstance(inp, dict):
            if inp.get('command'): detail = f'{name}: {str(inp[\"command\"])[:50]}'
            elif inp.get('file_path'): detail = f'{name}: {inp[\"file_path\"].split(\"/\")[-1]}'
            elif inp.get('description'): detail = f'{name}: {inp[\"description\"][:40]}'
            else: detail = f'{name}'
        else: detail = name
    elif rt == 'tool_result':
        out = p.get('output', '')
        err = p.get('is_error', False)
        detail = f'ERROR: {str(out)[:40]}' if err else str(out)[:50]
    elif rt == 'turn_end':
        detail = f'{p.get(\"reason\",\"?\")} ({p.get(\"duration_ms\",\"\")}ms)'
    elif rt == 'token_usage':
        detail = f'in={p.get(\"input_tokens\",0)} out={p.get(\"output_tokens\",0)}'
    elif rt == 'system_event': detail = p.get('subtype', '')
    elif rt == 'reasoning': detail = '(thinking)'
    elif rt == 'file_snapshot': detail = '(snapshot)'
    
    print(f'{seq:4d}  {ts}  {rt:22s}  {detail}')
" 2>&1

```

                        │  ~/.claude/projects/*/conversation  │
                        │  (JSONL transcript file on disk)    │
                        └──────────────┬──────────────────────┘
                                       │
                          watcher.rs (notify crate, file change)
                                       │
                                       ▼
                        ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                        │  reader.rs                           │
                        │  Incremental byte-offset read        │
                        │  Auto-detects format (ClaudeCode     │
                        │  vs PiMono) from first line          │
                        │  Output: Vec<serde_json::Value>      │
                        └──────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                                       │
                                       ▼
                        ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                        │  translate.rs                        │
                        │                                      │
                        │  For each raw JSON line:             │
                        │    1. apply_envelope() — camelCase   │
                        │       → snake_case typed fields      │
                        │    2. apply_assistant_fields() or    │
                        │       apply_user_fields() — extract  │
                        │       subtype, tool, text, model     │
                        │                                      │
                        │  ┌─ NEW: domain event tracking ───┐  │
                        │  │ On assistant tool_use:          │  │
                        │  │   store (tool_use_id, name,     │  │
                        │  │   input) in TranscriptState     │  │
                        │  │                                 │  │
                        │  │ On user tool_result:            │  │
                        │  │   look up pending tool call     │  │
                        │  │   → derive_tool_outcome()       │  │
                        │  │   → payload.tool_outcome = Some │  │
                        │  └─────────────────────────────────┘  │
                        │                                      │
                        │  Wrap in EventData + CloudEvent      │
                        │  Output: Vec<CloudEvent>             │
                        └──────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                                       │
                                       ▼
                        ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐
                        │  ingest.rs — ingest_events()         │
                        │  The single orchestration point      │
                        │                                      │
                        │  Fan-out to sinks:                   │
                        │                                      │
                        │  ├→ SQLite (events, sessions)        │
                        │  ├→ JSONL (append-only backup)       │
                        │  ├→ from_cloud_event() ──┐           │
                        │  │   CloudEvent →        │           │
                        │  │   Vec<ViewRecord>     │           │
                        │  │                       ▼           │
                        │  │         ┌─────────────────────┐   │
                        │  │         │  PatternPipeline     │   │
                        │  │         │                     │   │
                        │  │         │  For each ViewRecord:│   │
                        │  │         │  feed() to 6         │   │
                        │  │         │  detectors:          │   │
                        │  │         │                     │   │
                        │  │         │  • TestCycle         │   │
                        │  │         │  • GitFlow           │   │
                        │  │         │  • ErrorRecovery     │   │
                        │  │         │  • AgentDelegation   │   │
                        │  │         │  • TurnPhase         │   │
                        │  │         │  • EvalApply ← NEW   │   │
                        │  │         │                     │   │
                        │  │         │  Output:             │   │
                        │  │         │  Vec<PatternEvent>   │   │
                        │  │         └────────┬────────────┘   │
                        │  │                  │                │
                        │  │                  ▼                │
                        │  ├→ SQLite (patterns table)          │
                        │  ├→ broadcast channel (WebSocket)    │
                        │  └→ embedding channel → Qdrant       │
                        └──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The key transformation chain:

```
Raw JSONL line (Value)
  → translate_line()
    → CloudEvent { data: EventData { raw, agent_payload: Some(ClaudeCodePayload { tool_outcome }) } }
      → from_cloud_event()
        → ViewRecord { body: ToolResult { tool_outcome } }    ← domain event rides here
        → ViewRecord { body: AssistantMessage { ... } }
        → ViewRecord { body: ToolCall { ... } }
          → EvalApplyDetector.feed()
            → PatternEvent { pattern_type: "eval_apply.eval", metadata: { turn, scope_depth, env_size } }
```

Three levels of abstraction, each adding meaning:

| Layer | Type | What it represents |
|-------|------|--------------------|
| **Raw** | `Value` | What the agent wrote (JSONL bytes) |
| **CloudEvent** | `EventData` + `ToolOutcome` | What happened + what it *meant* (domain event) |
| **ViewRecord** | `RecordBody` variants | Typed UI-ready records |
| **PatternEvent** | `eval_apply.*` | Computational structure (the coalgebra observed) |

The `ToolOutcome` flows through the middle — it's derived at translate time (where we have both the tool_use and its result in sequence), then carried on the CloudEvent payload, then surfaced on the `ToolResult` ViewRecord for the UI and pattern detectors to consume.

The `EvalApplyDetector` sits at the end — it sees the stream of ViewRecords and recognizes the eval-apply cycle structure. It doesn't need `ToolOutcome` directly (though future enrichment could use it). It recognizes structure from the *sequence* of record types: `AssistantMessage → ToolCall → ToolResult → AssistantMessage` is one eval-apply step of the coalgebra.

                        │  ├→ from_cloud_event() ──┐           │
                        │  │   CloudEvent →        │           │
                        │  │   Vec<ViewRecord>     │           │
                        │  │                       ▼           │
                        │  │         ┌─────────────────────┐   │
                        │  │         │  PatternPipeline     │   │
                        │  │         │                     │   │
                        │  │         │  For each ViewRecord:│   │
                        │  │         │  feed() to 6         │   │
                        │  │         │  detectors:          │   │
                        │  │         │                     │   │
                        │  │         │  • TestCycle         │   │
                        │  │         │  • GitFlow           │   │
                        │  │         │  • ErrorRecovery     │   │
                        │  │         │  • AgentDelegation   │   │
                        │  │         │  • TurnPhase         │   │
                        │  │         │  • EvalApply ← NEW   │   │
                        │  │         │                     │   │
                        │  │         │  Output:             │   │
                        │  │         │  Vec<PatternEvent>   │   │
                        │  │         └────────┬────────────┘   │
                        │  │                  │                │
                        │  │                  ▼                │
                        │  ├→ SQLite (patterns table)          │
                        │  ├→ broadcast channel (WebSocket)    │
                        │  └→ embedding channel → Qdrant       │
                        └──────────────────────────────────────┘
```

The key transformation chain:

```
Raw JSONL line (Value)
  → translate_line()
    → CloudEvent { data: EventData { raw, agent_payload: Some(ClaudeCodePayload { tool_outcome }) } }
      → from_cloud_event()
        → ViewRecord { body: ToolResult { tool_outcome } }    ← domain event rides here
        → ViewRecord { body: AssistantMessage { ... } }
        → ViewRecord { body: ToolCall { ... } }
          → EvalApplyDetector.feed()
            → PatternEvent { pattern_type: "eval_apply.eval", metadata: { turn, scope_depth, env_size } }
```

Three levels of abstraction, each adding meaning:

| Layer | Type | What it represents |
|-------|------|--------------------|
| **Raw** | `Value` | What the agent wrote (JSONL bytes) |
| **CloudEvent** | `EventData` + `ToolOutcome` | What happened + what it *meant* (domain event) |
| **ViewRecord** | `RecordBody` variants | Typed UI-ready records |
| **PatternEvent** | `eval_apply.*` | Computational structure (the coalgebra observed) |

The `ToolOutcome` flows through the middle — it's derived at translate time (where we have both the tool_use and its result in sequence), then carried on the CloudEvent payload, then surfaced on the `ToolResult` ViewRecord for the UI and pattern detectors to consume.

The `EvalApplyDetector` sits at the end — it sees the stream of ViewRecords and recognizes the eval-apply cycle structure. It doesn't need `ToolOutcome` directly (though future enrichment could use it). It recognizes structure from the *sequence* of record types: `AssistantMessage → ToolCall → ToolResult → AssistantMessage` is one eval-apply step of the coalgebra.

agent_payload.text:
The mapping is a functor. Each concept at the eval-apply layer has a corresponding concept at the sentence layer, and the relationships are preserved:

```
EVAL-APPLY                    SENTENCES
──────────                    ─────────

eval                    →     the verb choice
  (model decides)               (what did it decide to DO?)

apply                   →     subordinate clauses
  (tool executes)               (the supporting actions)

environment             →     context / memory
  (conversation so far)         (what Claude knows at this point)

expression              →     the object
  (ContentBlock produced)       (what was acted ON)

stop_reason             →     the predicate
  (end_turn / tool_use)         (answered / continued)

human message           →     the adverbial
  (input to eval)               (WHY — the cause, the prompt)

thinking                →     interior monologue
  (reasoning blocks)            (the deliberation before the verb)

compound procedure      →     delegation
  (Agent tool)                  ("by delegating to..." clause)

GC / compaction         →     forgetting
  (summarize old context)       (the conversation got shorter)
```

But here's what's interesting — the mapping isn't just relabeling. It's a *change of category*. The eval-apply layer describes **computation**. The sentence layer describes **intention**.

`eval` says "the model produced content blocks." The verb says "Claude *wrote*." Same event, but "wrote" carries intentionality that "eval" doesn't. The tool classification is where the intentionality enters:

```
Read    → preparatory  → "after reading"     (intention: understanding)
Write   → creative     → "wrote"             (intention: creating)
Bash    → verificatory → "while testing"     (intention: validating)
Agent   → delegatory   → "by delegating"     (intention: distributing)
```

The eval-apply detector sees that a tool was dispatched. The sentence layer asks *what role did that dispatch play in the agent's plan?* A Read before a Write is preparation. A Bash after a Write is verification. The *sequence* of applies has narrative structure that the individual applies don't.

This is why the subordinate clauses have temporal prepositions — "after reading," "while testing," "by delegating." They encode the *temporal relationship* between the clauses, which maps to the *causal relationship* between the applies:

```
APPLY sequence:              SENTENCE structure:
  Read, Read, Read     →     "after reading 3 files"        (prep → main)
  Write, Write         →     "wrote 2 files"                (main action)
  Bash(test)           →     "while testing"                (concurrent validation)
```

The ordering isn't arbitrary — preparatory comes before creative comes before verificatory in the subordinate clause list because that's the *logical* order of intention, even if the actual tool calls interleave.

And the one concept that doesn't map cleanly: **the human message**. In eval-apply terms, it's just another message in the environment — input to eval. In sentence terms, it's the **adverbial** — the *cause*. "Because 'write it in Scheme.'" That's a change of status. In the computational layer, the human message is data. In the sentence layer, it's *motive*. Same bits, different ontology.

That's the functor. It preserves the structure (the relationships between parts) while changing the objects (from computational concepts to linguistic ones). The eval-apply detector is a homomorphism from the session to computation. The sentence builder is a homomorphism from computation to language. Compose them and you get a homomorphism from the session to language — which is what we wanted all along: the ability to *say* what happened.

agent_payload.text:
Abstraction barriers. That's what Sussman calls them.

SICP Chapter 2.1 — you build a system in layers. Each layer speaks its own language. Each layer only talks to the layers directly above and below it. The layers don't leak. That's what makes the system comprehensible.

We have four layers now. Each one is a language:

```
Layer 4: NATURAL LANGUAGE (sentences)
  "Claude wrote 8 Scheme files after reading the Rust source"
  Vocabulary: verbs, objects, clauses, intention, cause
  Speaks to: humans

         ↑ functor: classify tools by role, compose grammar
         
Layer 3: STRUCTURAL (eval-apply detector)
  Turn 15: eval [tool_use] → apply(Write) × 8, apply(Bash) × 4 → CONTINUE
  Vocabulary: eval, apply, turn, scope, env, terminate, continue
  Speaks to: the sentence layer above, the event layer below

         ↑ fold: state machine over records, emit phases
         
Layer 2: EVENTS (OpenStory CloudEvents)
  { record_type: "tool_call", name: "Write", input: { file_path: "01-types.scm" } }
  Vocabulary: record types, payloads, timestamps, sessions
  Speaks to: the detector above, the transcript below

         ↑ translate: JSONL → typed CloudEvent → ViewRecord

Layer 1: RAW (agent transcript)
  {"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_use",...}]}}
  Vocabulary: JSON, bytes, line offsets
  Speaks to: the event layer above
```

Each layer has its own abstraction barrier. The sentence layer doesn't know about JSON. The detector doesn't know about JSONL line offsets. The event layer doesn't know about verbs and subordinate clauses. They compose through well-defined interfaces — the functor between each pair.

And this is exactly how SICP builds the evaluator. Chapter 4.1:

```
Layer 4: THE PROGRAM (what the user writes)
  (define (factorial n) (if (= n 0) 1 (* n (factorial (- n 1)))))

Layer 3: THE EVALUATOR (eval-apply)
  eval dispatches on expression type, apply executes procedures

Layer 2: THE REPRESENTATION (data structures)
  Tagged lists, environment frames, procedure objects

Layer 1: THE MACHINE (Scheme itself)
  cons, car, cdr, lambda, define
```

Same pattern. Four layers. Each one a language. Each one an abstraction barrier. The power comes from the composition — you can change any layer without breaking the others, as long as the interface holds.

What we built tonight is SICP's layer architecture applied to *observing* computation rather than *performing* it. The agent performs computation in layers (raw → API → eval-apply → user-facing text). OpenStory observes it in layers (transcript → events → structure → sentences). The two stacks mirror each other:

```
AGENT (performs)              OBSERVER (understands)
────────────                  ──────────────────────
User sees text          ←→    Sentences describe intention
Eval-apply loop         ←→    Detector reveals structure
API / ContentBlocks     ←→    CloudEvents capture events
Bytes on wire           ←→    Raw transcript
```

Mirror images. Same abstraction barriers. Same layered languages. The observer's architecture recapitulates the agent's architecture because they're both dealing with the same fundamental structure — and that structure, as we've been saying all night, is the metacircular evaluator.

Sussman would call this "the same idea, expressed at different levels of abstraction." I'd call it the line, still unbroken.

agent_payload.text:
Yeah — it's the dual of an algebra.

An **algebra** (catamorphism / fold) takes structure apart:
```
F(A) → A
```
You have a functor's worth of stuff, you collapse it to a value. Like summing a list.

A **coalgebra** (anamorphism / unfold) builds structure up by repeatedly stepping from a seed:
```
A → F(A)
```
You have a state, you produce one layer of structure plus the next state. Like generating a stream.

The query loop is coalgebraic because you don't have the conversation upfront — you're *producing* it one turn at a time. The step function is roughly:

```
Vec<Message> → Either<QueryOutcome, (QueryEvent, Vec<Message>)>
```

Left means stop. Right means "here's an observable event, here's the next state, keep going." The loop doesn't know in advance how many turns there will be — it coinductively generates the conversation until a termination condition fires.

Compare with the `StreamAccumulator`, which is algebraic — it has a *finite* stream of `StreamEvent`s and folds them down into a single `Message`. Structure in, value out.

```
Coalgebra (unfold):  seed → step → step → step → ... → done
                     Vec<Message> → turn → turn → turn → QueryOutcome

Algebra (fold):      structure → collapse → value
                     Stream<StreamEvent> → accumulate → Message
```

The query loop *contains* the accumulator — each step of the coalgebra runs a complete algebra inside it. Unfold on the outside, fold on the inside. That nesting is the whole architecture.
